steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content: A line with slope $m$ and y-intercept $b$ has equation $y=mx+b$. The graph shows a y-intercept of $5$, so $b=5$.

steps.title: Substitute the slope
steps.content:
The slope is $\frac{1}{4}$, so the equation is:
$$f(x)=\frac{1}{4}x+5$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice A matches both the slope and intercept. Choice B has the wrong intercept, choice C has intercept $-\frac{5}{4}$, and choice D has intercept $-5$. Choice A is correct.
